comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Implemented (commit ee4807ed8). _review_code resolves session key (uuid4 fallback); shim.review_code + CodeReviewRequest gain session_id/target_ticket; gate resolves-or-creates code-review: session:<id> artifact (idempotent), stamps verdict.session_id, emits, best-effort relates_to trailer link. Local: ruff+mypy green, 38 code-review tests pass (create+reuse, uuid fallback, passthrough). Awaiting Gerrit Verified before close.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Pushed to Gerrit as change 476 (stacked on 475). Awaiting Verified + LLM-Review.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
`rebar review-code` (the local/coding-agent path) reviews a diff but persists NOTHING — only the
Gerrit voter emits a `code_review` artifact. Without a local artifact there is no per-session memory
for the region-gated novelty floor (blameless-grindable-noctule) to converge against. This story
makes local `review-code` emit a session-keyed `code_review` artifact, reusing the payload/reader
shape from revenued-thickset-dassie.

## What
- **Resolve the local memory key.** Use the centralized
  `rebar._commands.session_id.resolve_session_id()` (precedence: `REBAR_SESSION_ID` →
  `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` → `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` → `SESSION_ID`; validated shared-across-subagents,
  unique-per-parallel-session).
  - **Fallback (approach A):** when it returns `None` (bare/headless, no SessionStart shim), mint a
    per-invocation `uuid4` (do NOT persist a shared file). This guarantees ISOLATION — no
    local→Gerrit bleed and no cross-session contamination — at the cost of making local convergence
    intentionally INERT for a bare invocation (each run gets a fresh key, so the reader finds no
    prior). Genuine per-session convergence arrives wherever a session lifecycle exists (native
    harness var or a SessionStart shim exporting `REBAR_SESSION_ID`).
- **Set `session_id` on the verdict.** The local path sets `verdict["session_id"] = session_key` so
  the payload (revenued-thickset-dassie's `build_payload`, which reads `verdict.get("session_id")`)
  carries it top-level. The typed memory key for the reader is `session:<session_key>`.
- **Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist.** Thread the resolved key down the local call chain
  `_review_code → shim.review_code → gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict` and emit a session-keyed
  artifact:
  - **Signature changes (in scope):** add `target_ticket: str | None = None` and
    `session_id: str | None = None` parameters to `code_review/shim.py::review_code(...)`, AND add a
    `session_id: str | None = None` field to the `CodeReviewRequest` dataclass in `gate_dispatch.py`
    (alongside the existing `target_ticket`). The shim forwards both onto
    `CodeReviewRequest(target_ticket=…, session_id=…, …)`. Without these the resolved key cannot
    reach the emit path.
  - **Create-or-reuse idempotency (session key = stable convergence point).** Before emitting, a
    local-emit helper resolves ONE artifact ticket per session key, mirroring
    `voter.emit_code_review_artifact` (voter.py ~199–220) EXACTLY:
    `title = f"code-review: session:{session_key}"`; scan
    `rebar.list_tickets(ticket_type="code_review", repo_root=repo_root)` for a title match and REUSE
    that artifact id; else `rebar.create_ticket("code_review", title, …)`. Then `sidecar.emit(verdict,
    target_ticket=artifact_id, …)`. So two sequential local reviews under one session id append
    sidecar events to the SAME artifact (AC4) instead of duplicating.
  - **`relates_to` link (best-effort).** Link the artifact `relates_to` the work ticket resolved
    from the head commit's `rebar-ticket:` trailer (via the existing `extract_ticket_refs` +
    `resolve_ticket_id`, exactly as `voter.emit_code_review_artifact` does) for searchability. No new
    `--ticket` CLI flag is added (out of scope). A trailerless/unresolved/ticketless review STILL
    persists (memory is keyed by session, not by ticket) — the only degradation is no cross-run
    match when the key differs; a missing/unresolved link is logged, never fails the review.
  - **Best-effort throughout:** artifact create/reuse/emit/link failures are logged and swallowed —
    they must NEVER fail the review (mirrors the voter + sidecar posture).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py` (`_review_code`: resolve key, thread it down),
  `src/rebar/llm/code_review/shim.py` (`review_code`: add `target_ticket`/`session_id` params),
  `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` (local-emit helper: create-or-reuse the session artifact
  + set `verdict["session_id"]`).
- Depends on the payload shape + reader (revenued-thickset-dassie) — specifically `build_payload`
  reading `verdict.get("session_id")`, which lands with c639; this story is reviewed/implemented
  AFTER c639 is committed on the stack, so that dependency exists in the tree.
- OUT of scope: the Gerrit voter path (already emits; inherits the payload extension); the
  region_gated_floor consumer (blameless-grindable-noctule).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Local `rebar review-code` emits a `code_review` artifact keyed `session:<id>` carrying the
      reviewed-file `deps` map and a top-level `session_id`.
- [ ] The session key = `resolve_session_id()`; when `None`, a per-invocation `uuid4` is used (no
      shared-file persistence), making bare-invocation local convergence intentionally inert.
- [ ] `shim.review_code` gains `target_ticket` + `session_id` parameters and `CodeReviewRequest`
      gains a `session_id` field, both forwarded, so the local path can supply the artifact anchor
      and session key.
- [ ] Create-or-reuse idempotency: two sequential local reviews under ONE session id append to the
      SAME artifact (title `code-review: session:{id}`), not duplicate tickets.
- [ ] The artifact `relates_to`-links the ticket resolved from the head commit's `rebar-ticket:`
      trailer when one resolves; a trailerless/unresolved review still persists (keyed by session)
      and never fails the review (no new `--ticket` flag).
- [ ] Tests: a local review with a set `REBAR_SESSION_ID` emits/reuses one session-keyed artifact
      across two runs; a bare review (no session var) mints a uuid key and still persists; artifact
      failures never fail the review.
compiled_state.title: Persist a code_review artifact for local rebar review-code + session keying (uuid fallback)
